data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism requires that alternative accounting practices and merchant alphabets diffused widely enough to erode scribal monopolies across all four regions, yet the archaeological record shows that Linear B was confined to Crete and mainland Greece, while Hittite Anatolia and Ugarit used cuneiform scripts that were not replaced by alphabetic writing until after the collapse; the claim thus posits a uniform information-technology shift across polities with fundamentally different scribal traditions, which is unsupported by evidence.
